```{r apply-correction-to-maps, message=FALSE}
# Helper function pour afficher les changements
show_impact <- function(original, corrected, metric_name, unit) {
  mean_orig <- mean(original, na.rm = TRUE)
  mean_corr <- mean(corrected, na.rm = TRUE)
  delta <- mean_corr - mean_orig
  delta_pct <- (delta / mean_orig) * 100
  
  cat(sprintf("  %.1f %s → %.1f %s (Δ = %+.1f, %+.1f%%)\n",
              mean_orig, unit, mean_corr, unit, delta, delta_pct))
}

cat("Médiane d'aire:\n")
cat("  Detectree2: ")
show_impact(centro_D2$Med_Aire, centro_D2_corrected$Med_Aire, "Med", "m²")
cat("  SAM:        ")
show_impact(centro_SAM$Med_Aire, centro_SAM_corrected$Med_Aire, "Med", "m²")
cat("  D2S:        ")
show_impact(centro_D2S$Med_Aire, centro_D2S_corrected$Med_Aire, "Med", "m²")

cat("\nSomme d'aire:\n")
cat("  Detectree2: ")
show_impact(centro_D2$sum_Aire, centro_D2_corrected$sum_Aire, "Sum", "m²")
cat("  SAM:        ")
show_impact(centro_SAM$sum_Aire, centro_SAM_corrected$sum_Aire, "Sum", "m²")
cat("  D2S:        ")
show_impact(centro_D2S$sum_Aire, centro_D2S_corrected$sum_Aire, "Sum", "m²")

cat("\nDensité:\n")
cat("  Detectree2: ")
show_impact(centro_D2$Nb_ind, centro_D2_corrected$Nb_ind, "Dens", "ind/ha")
cat("  SAM:        ")
show_impact(centro_SAM$Nb_ind, centro_SAM_corrected$Nb_ind, "Dens", "ind/ha")
cat("  D2S:        ")
show_impact(centro_D2S$Nb_ind, centro_D2S_corrected$Nb_ind, "Dens", "ind/ha")

```

## Rasterisation des données corrigées

```{r rasterise-corrected, message=FALSE}

# Fonction de rasterisation (identique à votre code existant)
to_raster_df <- function(sf_points, field, r_template) {
  v  <- vect(sf_points)
  rr <- rasterize(v, r_template, field = field, fun = mean, na.rm = TRUE)
  crs(rr) <- crs(luki_tif)
  as.data.frame(rr, xy = TRUE) |>
    rename(value = 3) |>
    filter(!is.na(value))
}

# --- Liste des centroides CORRIGÉS ---
centro_list_corrected <- list(
  D2  = centro_D2_corrected,
  SAM = centro_SAM_corrected,
  D2S = centro_D2S_corrected
)

# Rasters par métrique (CORRIGÉS)
rasters_corrected <- lapply(names(centro_list_corrected), function(m) {
  list(
    Med_Aire = to_raster_df(centro_list_corrected[[m]], "Med_Aire", r) |> 
                mutate(Model = m, Metric = "Med_Aire"),
    sum_Aire = to_raster_df(centro_list_corrected[[m]], "sum_Aire", r) |> 
                mutate(Model = m, Metric = "sum_Aire"),
    Nb_ind   = to_raster_df(centro_list_corrected[[m]], "Nb_ind", r) |> 
                mutate(Model = m, Metric = "Nb_ind")
  )
})
names(rasters_corrected) <- names(centro_list_corrected)

df_Med_corrected <- bind_rows(lapply(rasters_corrected, `[[`, "Med_Aire"))
df_Sum_corrected <- bind_rows(lapply(rasters_corrected, `[[`, "sum_Aire"))
df_Nbi_corrected <- bind_rows(lapply(rasters_corrected, `[[`, "Nb_ind"))

# Limites pour les cartes corrigées
lims_Med_corrected <- quantile(df_Med_corrected$value, probs = c(0.025, 0.975), na.rm = TRUE)
lims_Sum_corrected <- quantile(df_Sum_corrected$value, probs = c(0.025, 0.975), na.rm = TRUE)
lims_Nbi_corrected <- quantile(df_Nbi_corrected$value, probs = c(0.025, 0.975), na.rm = TRUE)

cat("\n=== LIMITES POUR LES CARTES CORRIGÉES ===\n")
cat("Médiane: [", round(lims_Med_corrected[1], 1), ", ", 
    round(lims_Med_corrected[2], 1), "] m²\n", sep="")
cat("Somme:   [", round(lims_Sum_corrected[1], 0), ", ", 
    round(lims_Sum_corrected[2], 0), "] m²\n", sep="")
cat("Densité: [", round(lims_Nbi_corrected[1], 1), ", ", 
    round(lims_Nbi_corrected[2], 1), "] ind/ha\n", sep="")

```

## Visualisation : Panel complet des cartes corrigées

```{r panel-viridis-corrected, message=FALSE, fig.width=10, fig.height=10}

# Utiliser votre fonction plot_metric existante
# (elle est déjà définie dans votre chunk précédent)

# Titres de colonnes
column_titles_corr <- plot_grid(
  ggdraw() + draw_label("Detectree2", fontface = "bold", size = 12, x = 0.5, hjust = 0.5),
  ggdraw() + draw_label("SAM", fontface = "bold", size = 12, x = 0.5, hjust = 0.5),
  ggdraw() + draw_label("Detectree2SAM", fontface = "bold", size = 12, x = 0.5, hjust = 0.5),
  ncol = 3
)

# Création des trois cartes corrigées
map_Med_corr <- plot_metric(df_Med_corrected, "Median Area\n(corrected, m²)", 
                            lims_Med_corrected, show_x = FALSE)
map_Sum_corr <- plot_metric(df_Sum_corrected, "Sum Area\n(corrected, m²)", 
                            lims_Sum_corrected, show_x = FALSE)
map_Nbi_corr <- plot_metric(df_Nbi_corrected, "Density\n(corrected, ind/ha)", 
                            lims_Nbi_corrected, show_x = TRUE)

# Combiner en panel
panel_viridis_corrected <- plot_grid(
  column_titles_corr,
  map_Med_corr, map_Sum_corr, map_Nbi_corr,
  ncol = 1,
  rel_heights = c(0.05, 1, 1, 1),
  labels = c("", "A", "B", "C"),
  label_size = 14,
  align = "v",
  axis = "lr"
)

# Export
ggsave(
  filename = "D:/PhD/Data/Art1_rendu/Graphique/panel_viridis_CORRECTED.png",
  plot = panel_viridis_corrected,
  width = 10,
  height = 10,
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)

print(panel_viridis_corrected)

```

## Cartes des meilleures méthodes (CORRIGÉES)

```{r best-maps-corrected, message=FALSE, fig.width=10, fig.height=10}

# --- Filtrer pour les meilleures méthodes ---
df_Med_D2S_corr <- df_Med_corrected |> filter(Model == "D2S")
df_Sum_D2S_corr <- df_Sum_corrected |> filter(Model == "D2S")
df_Nbi_SAM_corr <- df_Nbi_corrected |> filter(Model == "SAM")

lims_Med_D2S_corr <- quantile(df_Med_D2S_corr$value, probs = c(0.025, 0.975), na.rm = TRUE)
lims_Sum_D2S_corr <- quantile(df_Sum_D2S_corr$value, probs = c(0.025, 0.975), na.rm = TRUE)
lims_Nbi_SAM_corr <- quantile(df_Nbi_SAM_corr$value, probs = c(0.025, 0.975), na.rm = TRUE)

# Emprise commune
all_xy_corr <- bind_rows(
  df_Med_D2S_corr[, c("x","y")],
  df_Sum_D2S_corr[, c("x","y")],
  df_Nbi_SAM_corr[, c("x","y")]
)
buf <- 150
xlim_sel_corr <- range(all_xy_corr$x, na.rm = TRUE) + c(-buf, buf)
ylim_sel_corr <- range(all_xy_corr$y, na.rm = TRUE) + c(-buf, buf)

# Créer les 3 cartes avec votre fonction plot_metric_single
map_Med_D2S_corr <- plot_metric_single(
  df_Med_D2S_corr,
  "Median Crown Area\n(corrected, m²)",
  lims_Med_D2S_corr,
  xlim_use = xlim_sel_corr,
  ylim_use = ylim_sel_corr
)

map_Sum_D2S_corr <- plot_metric_single(
  df_Sum_D2S_corr,
  "Total Crown Area\n(corrected, m²)",
  lims_Sum_D2S_corr,
  xlim_use = xlim_sel_corr,
  ylim_use = ylim_sel_corr
)

map_Nbi_SAM_corr <- plot_metric_single(
  df_Nbi_SAM_corr,
  "Crown Density\n(corrected, ind/ha)",
  lims_Nbi_SAM_corr,
  xlim_use = xlim_sel_corr,
  ylim_use = ylim_sel_corr
)

# Assemblage
top_row_corr <- plot_grid(
  map_Med_D2S_corr,
  map_Sum_D2S_corr,
  ncol = 2,
  align = "hv",
  axis = "tb",
  labels = c("A. Detectree2SAM (corrected)", "B. Detectree2SAM (corrected)"),
  label_size = 14,
  label_x = 0.1,
  label_y = 1
)

bottom_row_corr <- plot_grid(
  map_Nbi_SAM_corr,
  labels = "C. SAM (corrected)",
  label_size = 14,
  label_x = 0.35,
  label_y = 1.02
)

panel_best_corrected <- plot_grid(
  top_row_corr,
  bottom_row_corr,
  ncol = 1,
  rel_heights = c(1, 1)
)

# Export
ggsave(
  filename = "D:/PhD/Data/Art1_rendu/Graphique/panel_best_map_CORRECTED.png",
  plot = panel_best_corrected,
  width = 10,
  height = 10,
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)

print(panel_best_corrected)

# === Export cartes individuelles ===
ggsave("D:/PhD/Data/Art1_rendu/Graphique/map_Med_D2S_CORRECTED.png",
       plot = map_Med_D2S_corr, width = 2.5, height = 3, dpi = 300, bg = "white")

ggsave("D:/PhD/Data/Art1_rendu/Graphique/map_Sum_D2S_CORRECTED.png",
       plot = map_Sum_D2S_corr, width = 2.5, height = 3, dpi = 300, bg = "white")

ggsave("D:/PhD/Data/Art1_rendu/Graphique/map_Nbi_SAM_CORRECTED.png",
       plot = map_Nbi_SAM_corr, width = 2.5, height = 3, dpi = 300, bg = "white")

cat("\n✓ Toutes les cartes corrigées ont été générées!\n")
cat("  - Panel complet: panel_viridis_CORRECTED.png\n")
cat("  - Meilleures méthodes: panel_best_map_CORRECTED.png\n")
cat("  - 3 cartes individuelles\n")

```

## Cartes CMY des désaccords entre modèles (VALEURS CORRIGÉES)

> **Objectif :** Visualiser les désaccords spatiaux entre méthodes **après correction linéaire**.
> La correction réduit-elle les désaccords locaux ? Les patterns changent-ils ?

```{r CMY-corrected}

# ==========================================
# PARAMÈTRES GLOBAUX (identiques)
# ==========================================
GAMMA <- 1
QUANTILES <- c(0, 1)
INTENSITY_FLOOR <- 1.5

# ==========================================
# 1. CALCUL DES QUANTILES SUR DONNÉES CORRIGÉES
# ==========================================
get_sd_range <- function(df, quantiles = QUANTILES) {
  df_wide <- df %>%
    tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = Model, values_from = value) %>%
    mutate(sd_val = apply(dplyr::select(., D2, SAM, D2S), 1, sd, na.rm = TRUE))
  quantile(df_wide$sd_val, quantiles, na.rm = TRUE)
}

# Calcul sur données CORRIGÉES
sd_range_med_corr <- get_sd_range(df_Med_corrected)
sd_range_sum_corr <- get_sd_range(df_Sum_corrected)
sd_range_nbi_corr <- get_sd_range(df_Nbi_corrected)

cat("=== SD RANGES (CORRIGÉES) ===\n")
cat(" - Med :", round(sd_range_med_corr, 3), "\n")
cat(" - Sum :", round(sd_range_sum_corr, 3), "\n")
cat(" - Nbi :", round(sd_range_nbi_corr, 3), "\n\n")

# Comparaison avec valeurs originales (si disponibles)
if (exists("sd_range_med")) {
  cat("=== COMPARAISON ORIGINAL vs CORRIGÉ ===\n")
  cat("Médiane - SD range original:", round(sd_range_med, 3), 
      "→ corrigé:", round(sd_range_med_corr, 3),
      sprintf("(%.1f%% réduction)\n", 
              ((sd_range_med[2] - sd_range_med_corr[2]) / sd_range_med[2]) * 100))
  cat("Somme   - SD range original:", round(sd_range_sum, 3), 
      "→ corrigé:", round(sd_range_sum_corr, 3),
      sprintf("(%.1f%% réduction)\n", 
              ((sd_range_sum[2] - sd_range_sum_corr[2]) / sd_range_sum[2]) * 100))
  cat("Densité - SD range original:", round(sd_range_nbi, 3), 
      "→ corrigé:", round(sd_range_nbi_corr, 3),
      sprintf("(%.1f%% réduction)\n\n", 
              ((sd_range_nbi[2] - sd_range_nbi_corr[2]) / sd_range_nbi[2]) * 100))
}

# ==========================================
# 2. FONCTIONS COMMUNES (inchangées)
# ==========================================
normalize_cmy <- function(c_raw, m_raw, y_raw) {
  c_raw[!is.finite(c_raw)] <- 0
  m_raw[!is.finite(m_raw)] <- 0  
  y_raw[!is.finite(y_raw)] <- 0
  max_val <- pmax(c_raw, pmax(m_raw, y_raw))
  max_val[max_val == 0 | !is.finite(max_val)] <- 1
  list(C = c_raw / max_val, M = m_raw / max_val, Y = y_raw / max_val)
}

calculate_intensity <- function(sd_values, sd_range, gamma = GAMMA, floor = INTENSITY_FLOOR) {
  I_raw <- scales::rescale(sd_values, to = c(0, 1), from = sd_range)
  I_raw[!is.finite(I_raw)] <- 0
  pmax(I_raw, floor) ^ gamma
}

cmy_to_rgb <- function(C, M, Y, I) {
  list(
    R = pmin(pmax((1 - C) * I, 0), 1),
    G = pmin(pmax((1 - M) * I, 0), 1),
    B = pmin(pmax((1 - Y) * I, 0), 1)
  )
}

create_cmy_map <- function(df_wide, sd_range) {
  df_processed <- df_wide %>%
    mutate(
      mean_val = ifelse(mean_val == 0 | !is.finite(mean_val), 1e-10, mean_val),
      C_raw = D2 / mean_val,
      M_raw = SAM / mean_val,
      Y_raw = D2S / mean_val
    )
  cmy <- normalize_cmy(df_processed$C_raw, df_processed$M_raw, df_processed$Y_raw)
  I <- calculate_intensity(df_processed$sd_val, sd_range)
  rgb_vals <- cmy_to_rgb(cmy$C, cmy$M, cmy$Y, I)
  df_processed %>%
    mutate(
      C = cmy$C, M = cmy$M, Y = cmy$Y, I = I,
      R = rgb_vals$R, G = rgb_vals$G, B = rgb_vals$B,
      hex = rgb(R, G, B)
    )
}

create_cmy_triangle <- function(sd_range, gamma = GAMMA, floor = INTENSITY_FLOOR, n = 200) {
  h <- sqrt(3)/2
  tri_grid <- expand.grid(x = seq(0, 1, length.out = n), y = seq(0, h, length.out = n))
  tri_grid <- tri_grid[
    tri_grid$y >= 0 &
    tri_grid$y <= h &
    tri_grid$x >= tri_grid$y / sqrt(3) &
    tri_grid$x <= 1 - tri_grid$y / sqrt(3), ]
  Y_raw <- (2 / sqrt(3)) * tri_grid$y
  C_raw <- tri_grid$x - 0.5 * Y_raw
  M_raw <- 1 - C_raw - Y_raw
  cmy <- normalize_cmy(C_raw, M_raw, Y_raw)
  sd_sim <- scales::rescale(tri_grid$y, to = sd_range, from = c(0, h))
  I <- calculate_intensity(sd_sim, sd_range, gamma, floor)
  rgb_vals <- cmy_to_rgb(cmy$C, cmy$M, cmy$Y, I)
  data.frame(
    x = tri_grid$x, y = tri_grid$y,
    C = cmy$C, M = cmy$M, Y = cmy$Y, I = I,
    hex = rgb(rgb_vals$R, rgb_vals$G, rgb_vals$B)
  )
}

make_triangle_plot <- function(tri_data, sd_range, title) {
  h <- sqrt(3)/2
  
  ggplot(tri_data, aes(x = x, y = y)) +
    geom_raster(aes(fill = hex)) +
    scale_fill_identity() +
    
    # Contours du triangle
    geom_segment(x = 0, y = 0, xend = 1, yend = 0, color = "black", size = 0.5, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
    geom_segment(x = 1, y = 0, xend = 0.5, yend = h, color = "black", size = 0.5, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
    geom_segment(x = 0.5, y = h, xend = 0, yend = 0, color = "black", size = 0.5, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
    
    # Lignes médianes
    geom_segment(x = 0.5, y = h/3, xend = 0, yend = 0, color = "white", linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.6) +
    geom_segment(x = 0.5, y = h/3, xend = 1, yend = 0, color = "white", linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.6) +
    geom_segment(x = 0.5, y = h/3, xend = 0.5, yend = h, color = "white", linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.6) +
    
    # Points (repères)
    geom_point(aes(x = 0.5, y = h/3), color = "grey", size = 1, inherit.aes = FALSE) +       
    geom_point(aes(x = 0.25, y = 0.144), color = "#3e3e3e", size = 1, inherit.aes = FALSE) + 
    geom_point(aes(x = 0.00, y = 0.00), color = "#3e3e3e", size = 1, inherit.aes = FALSE) + 
    geom_point(aes(x = 0.75, y = 0.144), color = "#3e3e3e", size = 1, inherit.aes = FALSE) + 
    geom_point(aes(x = 1, y = 0.00), color = "#3e3e3e", size = 1, inherit.aes = FALSE) +    
    geom_point(aes(x = 0.50, y = (2*h)/3), color = "#3e3e3e", size = 1, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
    geom_point(aes(x = 0.50, y = h*1), color = "#3e3e3e", size = 1, inherit.aes = FALSE) +  
    
    # Ajustement des marges pour laisser place aux labels
    coord_equal(xlim = c(-0.5, 1.5), ylim = c(-0.5, h + 0.5), expand = FALSE) +
    theme_void(base_size = 11) +
    
    # Labels des coins (légèrement décalés vers l'extérieur)
    annotate("text", x = 0, y = -0.06, label = "SAM", 
             hjust = 0.5, vjust = 1, size = 6, color = "black", fontface = "bold") +
    annotate("text", x = 1, y = -0.06, label = "Detectree2", 
             hjust = 0.5, vjust = 1, size = 6, color = "black", fontface = "bold") +
    annotate("text", x = 0.5, y = h + 0.12, label = "Detectree2SAM", 
             hjust = 0.5, vjust = 0, size = 6, color = "black", fontface = "bold") +
    
    # Annotations pour les % (inchangées)
    annotate("text", x = 0.50, y = (h/3)+0.02, color = "#3e3e3e", label = "100%", size = 6, hjust = 0.5, vjust = -0.75) +
    annotate("text", x = 0.25, y = h/6, color = "#3e3e3e", label = "50%", size = 6, hjust = 0.5, vjust = -0.5) +
    annotate("text", x = 0.06, y = 0.01, color = "#3e3e3e", label = "0%", size = 6, hjust = 0, vjust = -0.5) +
    
    annotate("text", x = 0.75, y = h/6, color = "#3e3e3e", label = "50%", size = 6, hjust = 0.5, vjust = -0.5) +
    annotate("text", x = 0.94, y = 0.01, color = "#3e3e3e", label = "0%", size = 6, hjust = 1, vjust = -0.5) +
    
    annotate("text", x = 0.5, y = (2*h)/3, color = "#3e3e3e", label = "50%", size = 6, hjust = 0.5, vjust = -0.5) +
    annotate("text", x = 0.5, y = h - 0.04, color = "#3e3e3e", label = "0%", size = 6, hjust = 0.5, vjust = -0.60) +
    
    labs(title = title) +
    theme(
      plot.background = element_blank(),
      panel.background = element_blank(),
      plot.margin = margin(5, 5, 5, 5),
      plot.title = element_text(color = "black", hjust = 0.5, size = 12, face = "bold")
    )
}

# ==========================================
# 3. CRÉATION DES CARTES CORRIGÉES
# ==========================================
prep_and_plot <- function(df, sd_range, title) {
  df_wide <- df %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = Model, values_from = value) %>%
    mutate(
      mean_val = rowMeans(across(c(D2, SAM, D2S)), na.rm = TRUE),
      sd_val = apply(across(c(D2, SAM, D2S)), 1, sd, na.rm = TRUE)
    )
  df_map <- create_cmy_map(df_wide, sd_range)
  tri_data <- create_cmy_triangle(sd_range, GAMMA, INTENSITY_FLOOR)
  p_map <- ggplot(df_map, aes(x, y)) +
    geom_raster(aes(fill = hex)) + 
    scale_fill_identity() +
    coord_fixed(ratio = 1) +
    labs(
      title = title,
      x = "Easting (m)",
      y = "Northing (m)"
    ) +
    theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
    theme(
     axis.line = element_blank(),
     panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
     plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
     panel.grid       = element_blank(),
     axis.text.x      = element_text(size = 5, color = "black"),
     axis.text.y      = element_text(size = 5, color = "black", angle = 90, hjust = 0.5),
     axis.title       = element_text(size = 7, color = "black"),
     axis.ticks       = element_line(color = "black", linewidth = 0.3),
     plot.title       = element_text(color = "black", hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")
    )
  list(map = p_map, triangle = tri_data)
}

# --- Création des cartes CORRIGÉES ---
res_med_corr <- prep_and_plot(df_Med_corrected, sd_range_med_corr, "Median Crown Area")
res_sum_corr <- prep_and_plot(df_Sum_corrected, sd_range_sum_corr, "Sum Crown Area")
res_nbi_corr <- prep_and_plot(df_Nbi_corrected, sd_range_nbi_corr, "Crown Density")

# Enlever les titres
res_med_corr$map <- res_med_corr$map + labs(title = NULL)
res_sum_corr$map <- res_sum_corr$map + labs(title = NULL)
res_nbi_corr$map <- res_nbi_corr$map + labs(title = NULL)

# --- Triangle unique (global) ---
sd_range_global_corr <- range(c(sd_range_med_corr, sd_range_sum_corr, sd_range_nbi_corr))
tri_data_corr <- create_cmy_triangle(sd_range = sd_range_global_corr, GAMMA, INTENSITY_FLOOR)

triangle_plot_corr <- make_triangle_plot(
  tri_data_corr,
  sd_range_global_corr,
  title = " "
)

# --- 4) Organisation des cartes en lignes/colonnes ---
top_row_corr <- plot_grid(
  res_med_corr$map, res_sum_corr$map,
  ncol = 2,
  labels = c("A", "B"),
  label_size = 16,
  label_fontface = "bold",
  label_x = c(0.02, 0.02),
  label_y = c(0.98, 0.98)
)

bottom_row_corr <- plot_grid(
  res_nbi_corr$map,
  labels = "C",
  label_size = 16,
  label_x = 0.02,
  label_y = 1.05
)

# --- 5) Combiner cartes + triangle ---
final_panel_corr <- plot_grid(
  top_row_corr,
  plot_grid(bottom_row_corr, triangle_plot_corr, ncol = 2, rel_widths = c(0.5, 0.5)),
  ncol = 1,
  rel_heights = c(1, 1)
)

# --- 6) Export ---
ggsave(
  filename = "D:/PhD/Data/Art1_rendu/Graphique/CMY_map_triangle_CORRECTED.png",
  plot = final_panel_corr,
  width = 10,
  height = 10,
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)

cat("\n✓ Panel CMY corrigé sauvegardé: CMY_map_triangle_CORRECTED.png\n")

```

## Export des cartes CMY individuelles (CORRIGÉES)

```{r export-cmy-corrected}

# === Export des cartes CMY corrigées ===
ggsave("D:/PhD/Data/Art1_rendu/Graphique/map_Med_CMY_CORRECTED.png",
       plot = res_med_corr$map, width = 2.5, height = 3, dpi = 300, bg = "white")

ggsave("D:/PhD/Data/Art1_rendu/Graphique/map_Sum_CMY_CORRECTED.png",
       plot = res_sum_corr$map, width = 2.5, height = 3, dpi = 300, bg = "white")

ggsave("D:/PhD/Data/Art1_rendu/Graphique/map_Nbi_CMY_CORRECTED.png",
       plot = res_nbi_corr$map, width = 2.5, height = 3, dpi = 300, bg = "white")

# === Export du triangle CMY corrigé ===
ggsave("D:/PhD/Data/Art1_rendu/Graphique/triangle_CMY_CORRECTED.png",
       plot = triangle_plot_corr, width = 5, height = 5, dpi = 300, bg = "white")

cat("\n✓ Cartes CMY individuelles corrigées sauvegardées:\n")
cat("  - map_Med_CMY_CORRECTED.png\n")
cat("  - map_Sum_CMY_CORRECTED.png\n")
cat("  - map_Nbi_CMY_CORRECTED.png\n")
cat("  - triangle_CMY_CORRECTED.png\n")

```

## Statistiques de réduction des désaccords

```{r cmy-disagreement-stats}

# ==========================================
# ANALYSE QUANTITATIVE DES CHANGEMENTS
# ==========================================

cat("\n=== ANALYSE DES DÉSACCORDS (ORIGINAL vs CORRIGÉ) ===\n\n")

# Fonction pour calculer les stats de désaccord
calc_disagreement_stats <- function(df_original, df_corrected, metric_name) {
  
  # Original
  df_wide_orig <- df_original %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = Model, values_from = value) %>%
    mutate(
      mean_val = rowMeans(across(c(D2, SAM, D2S)), na.rm = TRUE),
      sd_val = apply(across(c(D2, SAM, D2S)), 1, sd, na.rm = TRUE),
      cv_val = sd_val / mean_val * 100  # Coefficient de variation
    )
  
  # Corrigé
  df_wide_corr <- df_corrected %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = Model, values_from = value) %>%
    mutate(
      mean_val = rowMeans(across(c(D2, SAM, D2S)), na.rm = TRUE),
      sd_val = apply(across(c(D2, SAM, D2S)), 1, sd, na.rm = TRUE),
      cv_val = sd_val / mean_val * 100
    )
  
  # Statistiques
  cat(sprintf("=== %s ===\n", metric_name))
  cat(sprintf("SD moyen original:  %.2f\n", mean(df_wide_orig$sd_val, na.rm = TRUE)))
  cat(sprintf("SD moyen corrigé:   %.2f\n", mean(df_wide_corr$sd_val, na.rm = TRUE)))
  cat(sprintf("Réduction SD:       %.1f%%\n\n", 
              (1 - mean(df_wide_corr$sd_val, na.rm = TRUE) / 
               mean(df_wide_orig$sd_val, na.rm = TRUE)) * 100))
  
  cat(sprintf("CV moyen original:  %.2f%%\n", mean(df_wide_orig$cv_val, na.rm = TRUE)))
  cat(sprintf("CV moyen corrigé:   %.2f%%\n", mean(df_wide_corr$cv_val, na.rm = TRUE)))
  cat(sprintf("Réduction CV:       %.1f%%\n\n", 
              (1 - mean(df_wide_corr$cv_val, na.rm = TRUE) / 
               mean(df_wide_orig$cv_val, na.rm = TRUE)) * 100))
  
  # Retourner les données pour analyse ultérieure
  return(list(
    original = df_wide_orig,
    corrected = df_wide_corr
  ))
}

# Calculer pour chaque métrique (si données originales disponibles)
if (exists("df_Med") && exists("df_Sum") && exists("df_Nbi")) {
  stats_med <- calc_disagreement_stats(df_Med, df_Med_corrected, "MÉDIANE D'AIRE")
  stats_sum <- calc_disagreement_stats(df_Sum, df_Sum_corrected, "SOMME D'AIRE")
  stats_nbi <- calc_disagreement_stats(df_Nbi, df_Nbi_corrected, "DENSITÉ")
} else {
  cat("⚠️ Données originales non disponibles pour comparaison\n")
}

cat(paste(rep("=", 70), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("INTERPRÉTATION:\n")
cat("- Réduction de SD: La correction réduit les écarts ABSOLUS entre méthodes\n")
cat("- Réduction de CV: La correction réduit les écarts RELATIFS (normalisés)\n")
cat("- Les cartes CMY corrigées montrent donc MOINS de désaccord spatial\n")
cat(paste(rep("=", 70), collapse = ""), "\n")

```

# 4. Description de Luki à l'échelle du paysage

```{r prep dataset}
# --- Aire de couronne (toutes les couronnes)
df_area_long <- bind_rows(
  Detectree2 |> st_drop_geometry() |> dplyr::select(area_m2) |> mutate(Model = "Detectree2"),
  SAM |> st_drop_geometry() |> dplyr::select(area_m2) |> mutate(Model = "SAM"),
  D2S |> st_drop_geometry() |> dplyr::select(area_m2) |> mutate(Model = "D2S")
)
# --- Indice de circularité (exemple : 4*pi*Area / Perimeter^2)
df_SI_long <- bind_rows(
  Detectree2 |> st_drop_geometry() |> dplyr::select(SI) |> mutate(Model = "Detectree2"),
  SAM |> st_drop_geometry() |> dplyr::select(SI) |> mutate(Model = "SAM"),
  D2S |> st_drop_geometry() |> dplyr::select(SI) |> mutate(Model = "D2S")
)
if ("D2" %in% unique(df_Nbi$Model)) {
  df_Nbi$Model[df_Nbi$Model == "D2"] <- "Detectree2"
}
if ("D2" %in% unique(df_Med$Model)) {
  df_Med$Model[df_Med$Model == "D2"] <- "Detectree2"
}
if ("D2" %in% unique(df_Sum$Model)) {
  df_Sum$Model[df_Sum$Model == "D2"] <- "Detectree2"
}
# === Création des graphiques avec couleurs et axe x modifiable ===
xlim_area <- c(0, 400)  # <--- MODIFIE CETTE VALEUR POUR ZOOMER SUR L'AXE X
xlim_SI   <- c(0.6, 1)    # Shape Index entre 0 et 1
xlim_Nbi  <- c(0, 200)  # <--- Modifie selon tes valeurs
xlim_Med  <- c(0, 400)  # <--- Modifie selon tes valeurs
xlim_Sum  <- c(0, 9000)  # <--- Modifie selon tes valeurs
# === Palette CMY pastel adaptée pour fond blanc ===
model_colors_pastel <- c(
  "Detectree2" = "#33CCCC",  # Cyan un peu plus foncé
  "SAM"        = "#CC33CC",  # Magenta un peu plus foncé
  "D2S"        = "#CCAA00"   # Jaune doré pour rester lisible sur fond blanc
)

# === Distribution de l'aire des couronnes ===
plot_area <- ggplot(df_area_long, aes(x = area_m2, fill = Model, color = Model)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.3, linewidth = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = model_colors_pastel, name = "Method") +
  scale_color_manual(values = model_colors_pastel, name = "Method") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = xlim_area)+
  labs(x = "Canopy Crown Area (m²)", y = "Density") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    legend.position  = "top"
  )

# === Distribution du Shape Index (SI) ===
plot_SI <- ggplot(df_SI_long, aes(x = SI, fill = Model, color = Model)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.3, linewidth = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = model_colors_pastel, name = "Method") +
  scale_color_manual(values = model_colors_pastel, name = "Method") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = xlim_SI)+
  labs(x = "Shape Index (SI)", y = "Density") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    legend.position  = "top"
  )

# === Distribution de la densité de couronnes ===
plot_Nbi <- ggplot(df_Nbi, aes(x = value, fill = Model, color = Model)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.3, linewidth = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = model_colors_pastel, name = "Method") +
  scale_color_manual(values = model_colors_pastel, name = "Method") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = xlim_Nbi)+
  labs(x = "Canopy Crown Density (ind/ha)", y = "Density") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    legend.position  = "top"
  )

# === Distribution de la Mediane des top 20 couronnes ===
plot_Med_area <- ggplot(df_Med, aes(x = value, fill = Model, color = Model)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.3, linewidth = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = model_colors_pastel, name = "Method") +
  scale_color_manual(values = model_colors_pastel, name = "Method") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = xlim_Med)+
  labs(x = "Median Crown Area of the Top 20 Crowns/ha (m²)", y = "Density") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    legend.position  = "top"
  )

# === Distribution de la somme des top 20 couronnes ===
plot_Sum_Aire <- ggplot(df_Sum, aes(x = value, fill = Model, color = Model)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.3, linewidth = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = model_colors_pastel, name = "Method") +
  scale_color_manual(values = model_colors_pastel, name = "Method") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = xlim_Sum)+
  labs(x = "Sum Crown Area of the Top 20 Crowns/ha (m²)", y = "Density") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    legend.position  = "top"
  )

# === Combiner avec patchwork pour avoir UNE seule légende ===
panel_distributions <- (
  plot_area + plot_SI + plot_Med_area + plot_Sum_Aire + plot_Nbi
) +
  plot_layout(ncol = 1, guides = "collect") &
  theme(legend.position = "top")

# === Ajouter les labels A, B, C, D, E ===
panel_distributions <- panel_distributions +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "A") &  # Ajoute A, B, C, ...
  theme(plot.tag = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"))  # Style des tags

# Export
ggsave(
  filename = "D:/PhD/Data/Art1_rendu/Graphique/destriptive_Luki.png",
  plot = panel_distributions,
  width = 8,
  height = 16,
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)
```

# 5. Description de Luki à l'échelle du paysage (avec valeurs corrigées disponibles)

> **Note :** On ne peut corriger que les métriques pour lesquelles nous avons calculé 
> des facteurs correctifs (Médiane Top20, Somme Top20, Densité). Les distributions 
> d'aires individuelles et de Shape Index restent inchangées.

```{r prep-distributions-partial-correction}

# ============================================================================
# DONNÉES DISPONIBLES POUR DISTRIBUTIONS
# ============================================================================

cat("=== MÉTRIQUES DISPONIBLES ===\n\n")

cat("AVEC correction:\n")
cat("  - df_Med_corrected (Médiane Top20 par pixel)\n")
cat("  - df_Sum_corrected (Somme Top20 par pixel)\n")
cat("  - df_Nbi_corrected (Densité par pixel)\n\n")

cat("SANS correction (pas de facteurs disponibles):\n")
cat("  - df_area_long (Aires individuelles de toutes les couronnes)\n")
cat("  - df_SI_long (Shape Index de toutes les couronnes)\n\n")

# ============================================================================
# RENOMMER "D2" EN "Detectree2" SI NÉCESSAIRE
# ============================================================================

# Pour les données corrigées
if (exists("df_Nbi_corrected") && "D2" %in% unique(df_Nbi_corrected$Model)) {
  df_Nbi_corrected$Model[df_Nbi_corrected$Model == "D2"] <- "Detectree2"
}
if (exists("df_Med_corrected") && "D2" %in% unique(df_Med_corrected$Model)) {
  df_Med_corrected$Model[df_Med_corrected$Model == "D2"] <- "Detectree2"
}
if (exists("df_Sum_corrected") && "D2" %in% unique(df_Sum_corrected$Model)) {
  df_Sum_corrected$Model[df_Sum_corrected$Model == "D2"] <- "Detectree2"
}

# Pour les données originales (si pas déjà fait)
if ("D2" %in% unique(df_area_long$Model)) {
  df_area_long$Model[df_area_long$Model == "D2"] <- "Detectree2"
}
if ("D2" %in% unique(df_SI_long$Model)) {
  df_SI_long$Model[df_SI_long$Model == "D2"] <- "Detectree2"
}

cat("✓ Noms de modèles standardisés\n\n")

# ============================================================================
# STATISTIQUES COMPARATIVES (ORIGINAL vs CORRIGÉ)
# ============================================================================

cat("=== COMPARAISON MOYENNES (ORIGINAL vs CORRIGÉ) ===\n\n")

compare_spatial_means <- function(df_orig, df_corr, metric_name, unit) {
  cat(sprintf("%s:\n", metric_name))
  models <- c("Detectree2", "SAM", "D2S")
  for (m in models) {
    mean_orig <- mean(df_orig$value[df_orig$Model == m], na.rm = TRUE)
    mean_corr <- mean(df_corr$value[df_corr$Model == m], na.rm = TRUE)
    delta <- mean_corr - mean_orig
    delta_pct <- (delta / mean_orig) * 100
    cat(sprintf("  %s: %.1f %s → %.1f %s (Δ = %+.1f, %+.1f%%)\n",
                m, mean_orig, unit, mean_corr, unit, delta, delta_pct))
  }
  cat("\n")
}

# Comparaisons disponibles
if (exists("df_Med") && exists("df_Med_corrected")) {
  compare_spatial_means(df_Med, df_Med_corrected, "Médiane Top20", "m²")
}

if (exists("df_Sum") && exists("df_Sum_corrected")) {
  compare_spatial_means(df_Sum, df_Sum_corrected, "Somme Top20", "m²")
}

if (exists("df_Nbi") && exists("df_Nbi_corrected")) {
  compare_spatial_means(df_Nbi, df_Nbi_corrected, "Densité", "ind/ha")
}

```

## Panel comparatif : Original vs Corrigé (seulement pour métriques corrigées)

```{r comparison-overlaid, fig.width=10, fig.height=12}

# ============================================================================
# COMPARAISON SUPERPOSÉE : ORIGINAL (pointillé) + CORRIGÉ (solide)
# ============================================================================

# --- Préparer les données combinées ---

# Médiane Top20
df_Med_combined <- bind_rows(
  df_Med %>% mutate(Version = "Original"),
  df_Med_corrected %>% mutate(Version = "Corrected")
)

# Somme Top20
df_Sum_combined <- bind_rows(
  df_Sum %>% mutate(Version = "Original"),
  df_Sum_corrected %>% mutate(Version = "Corrected")
)

# Densité
df_Nbi_combined <- bind_rows(
  df_Nbi %>% mutate(Version = "Original"),
  df_Nbi_corrected %>% mutate(Version = "Corrected")
)

# ============================================================================
# GRAPHIQUES SUPERPOSÉS
# ============================================================================

# Palette de couleurs (identique)
model_colors_pastel <- c(
  "Detectree2" = "#33CCCC",
  "SAM"        = "#CC33CC",
  "D2S"        = "#CCAA00"
)

# --- A. CANOPY CROWN AREA (ORIGINAL - pas de correction disponible) ---
plot_area_orig <- ggplot(df_area_long, aes(x = area_m2, fill = Model, color = Model)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.3, linewidth = 1.2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = model_colors_pastel, name = "Method") +
  scale_color_manual(values = model_colors_pastel, name = "Method") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = xlim_area) +
  labs(
    title = "A.",
    x = "Crown Area (m²)",
    y = "Density"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    legend.position  = "top",
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0)
  ) +
  annotate("text", x = xlim_area[2] * 0.98, y = Inf, 
           label = "No correction\n(individual crowns)",
           hjust = 1, vjust = 1.5, size = 3, color = "gray30", fontface = "italic")

# --- B. SHAPE INDEX (ORIGINAL - pas de correction disponible) ---
plot_SI_orig <- ggplot(df_SI_long, aes(x = SI, fill = Model, color = Model)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.3, linewidth = 1.2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = model_colors_pastel, name = "Method") +
  scale_color_manual(values = model_colors_pastel, name = "Method") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = xlim_SI) +
  labs(
    title = "B.",
    x = "Shape Index (SI)",
    y = "Density"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    legend.position  = "none",
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0)
  ) +
  annotate("text", x = xlim_SI[2] * 0.98, y = Inf, 
           label = "No correction\n(shape metric)",
           hjust = 1, vjust = 1.5, size = 3, color = "gray30", fontface = "italic")

# --- C. MÉDIANE TOP20 (AVEC CORRECTION) ---
plot_Med_overlay <- ggplot() +
  # COUCHE 1 : Original en pointillé coloré transparent (derrière)
  geom_density(data = df_Med, 
               aes(x = value, color = Model),
               linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.8, alpha = 0.4) +
  # COUCHE 2 : Corrigé avec remplissage coloré opaque (devant)
  geom_density(data = df_Med_corrected,
               aes(x = value, fill = Model, color = Model),
               alpha = 0.3, linewidth = 1.2, linetype = "solid") +
  # Échelles
  scale_fill_manual(values = model_colors_pastel, name = "Method") +
  scale_color_manual(values = model_colors_pastel, name = "Method") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = xlim_Med) +
  labs(
    title = "C. ",
    x = "Median Crown Area of Top 20 Crowns/ha (m²)",
    y = "Density"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    legend.position  = "none",
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0)
  ) +
  # Annotation pour expliquer les styles
  annotate("text", x = xlim_Med[2] * 0.98, y = Inf, 
           label = "Dashed = Original\nSolid = Corrected",
           hjust = 1, vjust = 1.5, size = 3, color = "gray30")

# --- D. SOMME TOP20 (AVEC CORRECTION) ---
plot_Sum_overlay <- ggplot() +
  # Original en pointillé coloré transparent
  geom_density(data = df_Sum, 
               aes(x = value, color = Model),
               linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.8, alpha = 0.4) +
  # Corrigé avec remplissage coloré opaque
  geom_density(data = df_Sum_corrected,
               aes(x = value, fill = Model, color = Model),
               alpha = 0.3, linewidth = 1.2, linetype = "solid") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = model_colors_pastel, name = "Method") +
  scale_color_manual(values = model_colors_pastel, name = "Method") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = xlim_Sum) +
  labs(
    title = "D.",
    x = "Sum Crown Area of Top 20 Crowns/ha (m²)",
    y = "Density"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    legend.position  = "none",
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0)
  ) +
  annotate("text", x = xlim_Sum[2] * 0.98, y = Inf, 
           label = "Dashed = Original\nSolid = Corrected",
           hjust = 1, vjust = 1.5, size = 3, color = "gray30")

# --- E. DENSITÉ (AVEC CORRECTION) ---
plot_Nbi_overlay <- ggplot() +
  # Original en pointillé coloré transparent
  geom_density(data = df_Nbi, 
               aes(x = value, color = Model),
               linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.8, alpha = 0.4) +
  # Corrigé avec remplissage coloré opaque
  geom_density(data = df_Nbi_corrected,
               aes(x = value, fill = Model, color = Model),
               alpha = 0.3, linewidth = 1.2, linetype = "solid") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = model_colors_pastel, name = "Method") +
  scale_color_manual(values = model_colors_pastel, name = "Method") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = xlim_Nbi) +
  labs(
    title = "E.",
    x = "Canopy Crown Density (ind/ha)",
    y = "Density"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    legend.position  = "none",
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0)
  ) +
  annotate("text", x = xlim_Nbi[2] * 0.98, y = Inf, 
           label = "Dashed = Original\nSolid = Corrected",
           hjust = 1, vjust = 1.5, size = 3, color = "gray30")

# ============================================================================
# ASSEMBLAGE AVEC LÉGENDE COMMUNE
# ============================================================================

# Combiner les CINQ graphiques
panel_overlay_complete <- plot_grid(
  plot_area_orig,
  plot_SI_orig,
  plot_Med_overlay,
  plot_Sum_overlay,
  plot_Nbi_overlay,
  ncol = 1,
  align = "v",
  axis = "lr"
)

# Affichage
print(panel_overlay_complete)

# Export
ggsave(
  filename = "D:/PhD/Data/Art1_rendu/Graphique/descriptive_Luki_COMPLETE.png",
  plot = panel_overlay_complete,
  width = 10,
  height = 18,  # Plus haut pour 5 graphiques
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)

cat("\n✓ Panel complet sauvegardé: descriptive_Luki_COMPLETE.png\n")
cat("  - A, B: Distributions originales (pas de correction disponible)\n")
cat("  - C, D, E: Comparaison avant/après correction\n")
cat("    → Lignes pointillées transparentes = Original\n")
cat("    → Lignes solides + remplissage = Corrigé\n")
# ============================================================================
# STATISTIQUES DESCRIPTIVES PAR MODÈLE ET VERSION
# ============================================================================

calc_stats <- function(df, value_col, label) {
  df %>%
    group_by(Model) %>%
    summarise(
      Median = round(median(.data[[value_col]], na.rm = TRUE), 1),
      SD     = round(sd(.data[[value_col]], na.rm = TRUE), 1),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(Metric = label, Version = "Original")
}

calc_stats_corr <- function(df, value_col, label) {
  df %>%
    group_by(Model) %>%
    summarise(
      Median = round(median(.data[[value_col]], na.rm = TRUE), 1),
      SD     = round(sd(.data[[value_col]], na.rm = TRUE), 1),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(Metric = label, Version = "Corrected")
}

stats_all <- bind_rows(
  # A - Crown Area (pas de correction)
  calc_stats(df_area_long, "area_m2", "Crown Area (m²)"),
  # B - Shape Index (pas de correction)
  calc_stats(df_SI_long, "SI", "Shape Index"),
  # C - Médiane Top20
  calc_stats(df_Med, "value", "Median Crown Area (m²)"),
  calc_stats_corr(df_Med_corrected, "value", "Median Crown Area (m²)"),
  # D - Somme Top20
  calc_stats(df_Sum, "value", "Sum Crown Area (m²)"),
  calc_stats_corr(df_Sum_corrected, "value", "Sum Crown Area (m²)"),
  # E - Densité
  calc_stats(df_Nbi, "value", "Density (ind/ha)"),
  calc_stats_corr(df_Nbi_corrected, "value", "Density (ind/ha)")
) %>%
  dplyr::select(Metric, Version, Model, Median, SD) %>%
  arrange(Metric, Version, Model)

kable(stats_all, caption = "Descriptive statistics per algorithm and version (Original vs Corrected)") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed")) %>%
  collapse_rows(columns = 1:2, valign = "top")
```

## Analyse quantitative des shifts de distribution

```{r distribution-shift-analysis}

# ============================================================================
# QUANTIFIER LE DÉPLACEMENT DES DISTRIBUTIONS
# ============================================================================

cat("\n=== ANALYSE DES SHIFTS DE DISTRIBUTION ===\n\n")

analyze_shift <- function(df_orig, df_corr, metric_name) {
  cat(sprintf("--- %s ---\n", metric_name))
  
  models <- c("Detectree2", "SAM", "D2S")
  
  for (m in models) {
    # Données
    orig <- df_orig$value[df_orig$Model == m]
    corr <- df_corr$value[df_corr$Model == m]
    
    # Statistiques
    mean_orig <- mean(orig, na.rm = TRUE)
    mean_corr <- mean(corr, na.rm = TRUE)
    median_orig <- median(orig, na.rm = TRUE)
    median_corr <- median(corr, na.rm = TRUE)
    sd_orig <- sd(orig, na.rm = TRUE)
    sd_corr <- sd(corr, na.rm = TRUE)
    
    # Calculs de shift
    shift_mean <- mean_corr - mean_orig
    shift_median <- median_corr - median_orig
    change_sd <- ((sd_corr - sd_orig) / sd_orig) * 100
    
    cat(sprintf("\n%s:\n", m))
    cat(sprintf("  Moyenne:  %.1f → %.1f (Δ = %+.1f)\n", 
                mean_orig, mean_corr, shift_mean))
    cat(sprintf("  Médiane:  %.1f → %.1f (Δ = %+.1f)\n", 
                median_orig, median_corr, shift_median))
    cat(sprintf("  SD:       %.1f → %.1f (%.1f%% change)\n", 
                sd_orig, sd_corr, change_sd))
  }
  cat("\n")
}

# Appliquer aux trois métriques
analyze_shift(df_Med, df_Med_corrected, "MÉDIANE TOP20")
analyze_shift(df_Sum, df_Sum_corrected, "SOMME TOP20")
analyze_shift(df_Nbi, df_Nbi_corrected, "DENSITÉ")

# ============================================================================
# CONVERGENCE ENTRE MÉTHODES
# ============================================================================

cat("=== CONVERGENCE INTER-ALGORITHMES ===\n\n")

analyze_convergence <- function(df_orig, df_corr, metric_name) {
  cat(sprintf("--- %s ---\n", metric_name))
  
  # Coefficient de variation entre méthodes (original)
  means_orig <- sapply(c("Detectree2", "SAM", "D2S"), function(m) {
    mean(df_orig$value[df_orig$Model == m], na.rm = TRUE)
  })
  cv_orig <- (sd(means_orig) / mean(means_orig)) * 100
  
  # Coefficient de variation entre méthodes (corrigé)
  means_corr <- sapply(c("Detectree2", "SAM", "D2S"), function(m) {
    mean(df_corr$value[df_corr$Model == m], na.rm = TRUE)
  })
  cv_corr <- (sd(means_corr) / mean(means_corr)) * 100
  
  # Range entre méthodes
  range_orig <- max(means_orig) - min(means_orig)
  range_corr <- max(means_corr) - min(means_corr)
  
  cat(sprintf("  CV inter-méthodes:    %.1f%% → %.1f%% (%.1f%% réduction)\n",
              cv_orig, cv_corr, ((cv_orig - cv_corr) / cv_orig) * 100))
  cat(sprintf("  Range inter-méthodes: %.1f → %.1f (%.1f%% réduction)\n\n",
              range_orig, range_corr, ((range_orig - range_corr) / range_orig) * 100))
}

analyze_convergence(df_Med, df_Med_corrected, "MÉDIANE TOP20")
analyze_convergence(df_Sum, df_Sum_corrected, "SOMME TOP20")
analyze_convergence(df_Nbi, df_Nbi_corrected, "DENSITÉ")

cat(paste(rep("=", 70), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("INTERPRÉTATION:\n")
cat("- Shift positif/négatif : direction du déplacement de la distribution\n")
cat("- Réduction du CV : les trois méthodes convergent vers des valeurs plus proches\n")
cat("- Réduction du Range : l'écart max-min entre méthodes diminue\n")
cat(paste(rep("=", 70), collapse = ""), "\n")

```

## Analyse quantitative des shifts de distribution

```{r distribution-shift-analysis}

# ============================================================================
# QUANTIFIER LE DÉPLACEMENT DES DISTRIBUTIONS
# ============================================================================

cat("\n=== ANALYSE DES SHIFTS DE DISTRIBUTION ===\n\n")

analyze_shift <- function(df_orig, df_corr, metric_name) {
  cat(sprintf("--- %s ---\n", metric_name))
  
  models <- c("Detectree2", "SAM", "D2S")
  
  for (m in models) {
    # Données
    orig <- df_orig$value[df_orig$Model == m]
    corr <- df_corr$value[df_corr$Model == m]
    
    # Statistiques
    mean_orig <- mean(orig, na.rm = TRUE)
    mean_corr <- mean(corr, na.rm = TRUE)
    median_orig <- median(orig, na.rm = TRUE)
    median_corr <- median(corr, na.rm = TRUE)
    sd_orig <- sd(orig, na.rm = TRUE)
    sd_corr <- sd(corr, na.rm = TRUE)
    
    # Calculs de shift
    shift_mean <- mean_corr - mean_orig
    shift_median <- median_corr - median_orig
    change_sd <- ((sd_corr - sd_orig) / sd_orig) * 100
    
    cat(sprintf("\n%s:\n", m))
    cat(sprintf("  Moyenne:  %.1f → %.1f (Δ = %+.1f)\n", 
                mean_orig, mean_corr, shift_mean))
    cat(sprintf("  Médiane:  %.1f → %.1f (Δ = %+.1f)\n", 
                median_orig, median_corr, shift_median))
    cat(sprintf("  SD:       %.1f → %.1f (%.1f%% change)\n", 
                sd_orig, sd_corr, change_sd))
  }
  cat("\n")
}

# Appliquer aux trois métriques
analyze_shift(df_Med, df_Med_corrected, "MÉDIANE TOP20")
analyze_shift(df_Sum, df_Sum_corrected, "SOMME TOP20")
analyze_shift(df_Nbi, df_Nbi_corrected, "DENSITÉ")

# ============================================================================
# CONVERGENCE ENTRE MÉTHODES
# ============================================================================

cat("=== CONVERGENCE INTER-ALGORITHMES ===\n\n")

analyze_convergence <- function(df_orig, df_corr, metric_name) {
  cat(sprintf("--- %s ---\n", metric_name))
  
  # Coefficient de variation entre méthodes (original)
  means_orig <- sapply(c("Detectree2", "SAM", "D2S"), function(m) {
    mean(df_orig$value[df_orig$Model == m], na.rm = TRUE)
  })
  cv_orig <- (sd(means_orig) / mean(means_orig)) * 100
  
  # Coefficient de variation entre méthodes (corrigé)
  means_corr <- sapply(c("Detectree2", "SAM", "D2S"), function(m) {
    mean(df_corr$value[df_corr$Model == m], na.rm = TRUE)
  })
  cv_corr <- (sd(means_corr) / mean(means_corr)) * 100
  
  # Range entre méthodes
  range_orig <- max(means_orig) - min(means_orig)
  range_corr <- max(means_corr) - min(means_corr)
  
  cat(sprintf("  CV inter-méthodes:    %.1f%% → %.1f%% (%.1f%% réduction)\n",
              cv_orig, cv_corr, ((cv_orig - cv_corr) / cv_orig) * 100))
  cat(sprintf("  Range inter-méthodes: %.1f → %.1f (%.1f%% réduction)\n\n",
              range_orig, range_corr, ((range_orig - range_corr) / range_orig) * 100))
}

analyze_convergence(df_Med, df_Med_corrected, "MÉDIANE TOP20")
analyze_convergence(df_Sum, df_Sum_corrected, "SOMME TOP20")
analyze_convergence(df_Nbi, df_Nbi_corrected, "DENSITÉ")

cat(paste(rep("=", 70), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("INTERPRÉTATION:\n")
cat("- Shift positif/négatif : direction du déplacement de la distribution\n")
cat("- Réduction du CV : les trois méthodes convergent vers des valeurs plus proches\n")
cat("- Réduction du Range : l'écart max-min entre méthodes diminue\n")
cat(paste(rep("=", 70), collapse = ""), "\n")

```
## Tableau récapitulatif des facteurs correctifs

> **Objectif :** Présenter de manière claire et transparente tous les facteurs de correction linéaire
> calculés selon Piñeiro et al. (2008) pour chaque métrique et chaque méthode.

```{r correction-factors-table}

# ============================================================================
# TABLEAU DES FACTEURS CORRECTIFS
# ============================================================================

cat("\n=== FACTEURS DE CORRECTION LINÉAIRE ===\n")
cat("Équation de correction : Observed = a + b × Predicted\n")
cat("Référence : Piñeiro et al. (2008)\n\n")

# Vérifier que correction_factors existe
if (!exists("correction_factors")) {
  cat("⚠️ ERREUR : L'objet 'correction_factors' n'existe pas.\n")
  cat("   Assurez-vous d'avoir exécuté le chunk 'appliquer correction' d'abord.\n")
} else {
  
  # Afficher le tableau brut
  cat("=== TABLEAU BRUT ===\n\n")
  print(correction_factors)
  cat("\n")
  
  # ============================================================================
  # VERSION FORMATÉE POUR PUBLICATION
  # ============================================================================
  
  cat("\n=== VERSION FORMATÉE COMPLÈTE ===\n\n")
  
  # Réorganiser et enrichir le tableau
  correction_factors_formatted <- correction_factors %>%
    mutate(
      # Équation complète
      Equation = sprintf("y = %.2f + %.2f·x", a, b),
      # Interprétation de la pente
      Slope_Interpretation = case_when(
        abs(b - 1) < 0.05 ~ "≈ 1 (bon accord)",
        b < 1 ~ "< 1 (sous-estimation)",
        b > 1 ~ "> 1 (sur-estimation)"
      ),
      # Interprétation de l'intercept
      Intercept_Interpretation = case_when(
        abs(a) < 5 ~ "≈ 0 (pas de biais constant)",
        a < 0 ~ "< 0 (biais négatif)",
        a > 0 ~ "> 0 (biais positif)"
      ),
      # Efficacité de la correction
      Correction_Efficiency = case_when(
        Gain_pct > 60 ~ "Excellente (>60%)",
        Gain_pct > 40 ~ "Bonne (40-60%)",
        Gain_pct > 20 ~ "Modérée (20-40%)",
        TRUE ~ "Faible (<20%)"
      )
    ) %>%
    dplyr::select(Metric, Method, a, b, Equation, 
           RMSD_before, RMSD_after, Gain_pct,
           Slope_Interpretation, Intercept_Interpretation, Correction_Efficiency)
  
  # Tableau formaté avec kable
  print(kable(correction_factors_formatted, 
        digits = 2,
        col.names = c("Métrique", "Méthode", 
                     "Intercept (a)", "Pente (b)", "Équation",
                     "RMSD avant", "RMSD après", "Gain (%)",
                     "Interprétation pente", "Interprétation intercept",
                     "Efficacité correction"),
        caption = "Facteurs de correction linéaire et efficacité (Observed = a + b × Predicted)") %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
                  font_size = 10) %>%
    column_spec(1, bold = TRUE) %>%
    column_spec(8, bold = TRUE, color = "white", background = spec_color(correction_factors_formatted$Gain_pct, 
                                                                          begin = 0.3, end = 0.9, 
                                                                          option = "viridis", direction = 1)) %>%
    collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "top"))
  
  # ============================================================================
  # TABLEAU SIMPLIFIÉ POUR MANUSCRIT
  # ============================================================================
  
  cat("\n=== VERSION SIMPLIFIÉE (POUR MANUSCRIT) ===\n\n")
  
  correction_factors_simple <- correction_factors %>%
    mutate(
      `Correction equation` = sprintf("y = %.2f + %.2f·x", a, b),
      `RMSD reduction (%)` = sprintf("%.1f%%", Gain_pct)
    ) %>%
    dplyr::select(Metric, Method, `Correction equation`, RMSD_before, RMSD_after, `RMSD reduction (%)`)
  
  print(kable(correction_factors_simple,
        digits = 2,
        col.names = c("Metric", "Method", "Correction equation", 
                     "RMSD (original)", "RMSD (corrected)", "RMSD reduction"),
        caption = "Linear bias correction factors and prediction accuracy improvement") %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover")) %>%
    column_spec(6, bold = TRUE) %>%
    collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "top"))
  
  # ============================================================================
  # TABLEAU ULTRA-COMPACT POUR TEXTE PRINCIPAL
  # ============================================================================
  
  cat("\n=== VERSION ULTRA-COMPACTE (IN-TEXT TABLE) ===\n\n")
  
  correction_factors_compact <- correction_factors %>%
    mutate(
      Correction = sprintf("y = %.1f + %.2f·x (Δ=%.0f%%)", a, b, Gain_pct)
    ) %>%
    dplyr::select(Metric, Method, Correction)
  
  print(kable(correction_factors_compact,
        col.names = c("Metric", "Method", "Correction (RMSD reduction)"),
        caption = "Bias correction summary") %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"),
                  font_size = 11) %>%
    collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "top"))
  
}

```

## Visualisation graphique des facteurs correctifs

```{r plot-correction-factors, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}

# ============================================================================
# GRAPHIQUE : PENTES ET INTERCEPTS
# ============================================================================

if (exists("correction_factors")) {
  
  # Préparer les données pour visualisation
  cf_plot <- correction_factors %>%
    mutate(
      Method_color = case_when(
        Method == "Detectree2" ~ "#33CCCC",
        Method == "SAM" ~ "#CC33CC",
        Method == "D2S" ~ "#CCAA00"
      )
    )
  
  # Graphique de la pente
  plot_slopes <- ggplot(cf_plot, aes(x = Method, y = b, fill = Method)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.8) +
    geom_col(alpha = 0.7, width = 0.6) +
    geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.3f", b)), 
              vjust = ifelse(cf_plot$b > 1, 1.5, -0.5), 
              size = 3.5, fontface = "bold") +
    facet_wrap(~ Metric, ncol = 3) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("Detectree2" = "#33CCCC", 
                                 "SAM" = "#CC33CC", 
                                 "D2S" = "#CCAA00")) +
    labs(
      title = "Correction Slopes (b coefficient)",
      subtitle = "Dashed line = ideal slope (b = 1)",
      x = NULL,
      y = "Slope (b)"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
    theme(
      legend.position = "none",
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray90"),
      strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
    )
  
  # Graphique de l'intercept
  plot_intercepts <- ggplot(cf_plot, aes(x = Method, y = a, fill = Method)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.8) +
    geom_col(alpha = 0.7, width = 0.6) +
    geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f", a)), 
              vjust = ifelse(cf_plot$a > 0, 1.5, -0.5), 
              size = 3.5, fontface = "bold") +
    facet_wrap(~ Metric, ncol = 3) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("Detectree2" = "#33CCCC", 
                                 "SAM" = "#CC33CC", 
                                 "D2S" = "#CCAA00")) +
    labs(
      title = "Correction Intercepts (a coefficient)",
      subtitle = "Dashed line = ideal intercept (a = 0)",
      x = NULL,
      y = "Intercept (a)"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
    theme(
      legend.position = "none",
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray90"),
      strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
    )
  
  # Combiner
  panel_correction_factors <- plot_grid(
    plot_slopes,
    plot_intercepts,
    ncol = 1,
    labels = c("A", "B"),
    label_size = 14
  )
  
  print(panel_correction_factors)
  
  # Export
  ggsave(
    filename = "D:/PhD/Data/Art1_rendu/Graphique/correction_factors_visualization.png",
    plot = panel_correction_factors,
    width = 10,
    height = 8,
    dpi = 300,
    bg = "white"
  )
  
  cat("\n✓ Visualisation des facteurs correctifs sauvegardée\n")
  
} else {
  cat("⚠️ correction_factors non disponible pour visualisation\n")
}

```

## Export du tableau pour le manuscrit

```{r export-correction-table}

# ============================================================================
# EXPORT EN DIFFÉRENTS FORMATS
# ============================================================================

if (exists("correction_factors")) {
  
  # Créer dossier si nécessaire
  tables_dir <- "D:/PhD/Data/Art1_rendu/Tables"
  if (!dir.exists(tables_dir)) {
    dir.create(tables_dir, recursive = TRUE)
  }
  
  # --- 1. CSV (pour traitement ultérieur) ---
  write.csv(correction_factors,
            file.path(tables_dir, "correction_factors.csv"),
            row.names = FALSE)
  cat("✓ CSV sauvegardé: correction_factors.csv\n")
  
  # --- 2. Version formatée pour manuscrit ---
  correction_factors_manuscript <- correction_factors %>%
    mutate(
      Equation = sprintf("y = %.2f + %.2f·x", a, b),
      `p-value` = sprintf("slope: %.3f; intercept: %.3f", b, a)
    ) %>%
    dplyr::select(Metric, Method, Equation, `p-value`)
  
  write.csv(correction_factors_manuscript,
            file.path(tables_dir, "correction_factors_manuscript.csv"),
            row.names = FALSE)
  cat("✓ CSV manuscrit sauvegardé: correction_factors_manuscript.csv\n")
  
  # --- 3. Version LaTeX (optionnel) ---
  if (require(xtable, quietly = TRUE)) {
    latex_table <- xtable(correction_factors_manuscript,
                          caption = "Linear bias correction factors",
                          label = "tab:correction-factors")
    
    sink(file.path(tables_dir, "correction_factors.tex"))
    print(latex_table, include.rownames = FALSE)
    sink()
    
    cat("✓ LaTeX table sauvegardée: correction_factors.tex\n")
  }
  
  cat("\n=== FICHIERS EXPORTÉS ===\n")
  cat("Dossier:", tables_dir, "\n")
  cat("  - correction_factors.csv (complet)\n")
  cat("  - correction_factors_manuscript.csv (simplifié)\n")
  cat("  - correction_factors.tex (LaTeX, si xtable disponible)\n")
  
} else {
  cat("⚠️ correction_factors non disponible pour export\n")
}

```

## Interprétation des facteurs correctifs

```{r interpret-corrections}

# ============================================================================
# GUIDE D'INTERPRÉTATION
# ============================================================================

cat("\n")
cat(paste(rep("=", 70), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("GUIDE D'INTERPRÉTATION DES FACTEURS CORRECTIFS\n")
cat(paste(rep("=", 70), collapse = ""), "\n\n")

cat("ÉQUATION DE CORRECTION: Observed = a + b × Predicted\n\n")

cat("PENTE (b):\n")
cat("  - b = 1  → Prédictions parfaitement calibrées (variance correcte)\n")
cat("  - b < 1  → Modèle SURESTIME la variance (prédit trop dispersé)\n")
cat("  - b > 1  → Modèle SOUS-ESTIME la variance (prédit trop concentré)\n\n")

cat("INTERCEPT (a):\n")
cat("  - a = 0  → Pas de biais constant\n")
cat("  - a > 0  → Modèle SOUS-ESTIME systématiquement\n")
cat("  - a < 0  → Modèle SURESTIME systématiquement\n\n")

cat("TESTS STATISTIQUES:\n")
cat("  - p_slope < 0.05   → La pente diffère significativement de 1\n")
cat("  - p_intercept < 0.05 → L'intercept diffère significativement de 0\n\n")

cat("EXEMPLE D'INTERPRÉTATION:\n")
cat("  Si a = 50 et b = 1.2:\n")
cat("  → Le modèle sous-estime de 50 unités en moyenne (a > 0)\n")
cat("  → ET sous-estime la variance de 20% (b > 1)\n")
cat("  → Correction: y_corr = 50 + 1.2 × y_pred\n\n")

cat(paste(rep("=", 70), collapse = ""), "\n\n")

# ============================================================================
# RÉSUMÉ PAR MÉTRIQUE
# ============================================================================

if (exists("correction_factors")) {
  
  cat("=== RÉSUMÉ PAR MÉTRIQUE ===\n\n")
  
  for (metric in unique(correction_factors$Metric)) {
    cat(sprintf("--- %s ---\n", metric))
    
    subset <- correction_factors %>% filter(Metric == metric)
    
    for (i in 1:nrow(subset)) {
      row <- subset[i, ]
      
      # Interprétation de la pente
      slope_msg <- if (abs(row$b - 1) < 0.05) {
        "pente ≈ 1 (variance bien calibrée)"
      } else if (row$b < 1) {
        sprintf("pente = %.2f < 1 (surestime variance)", row$b)
      } else {
        sprintf("pente = %.2f > 1 (sous-estime variance)", row$b)
      }
      
      # Interprétation de l'intercept
      intercept_msg <- if (abs(row$a) < 5) {
        "intercept ≈ 0 (pas de biais)"
      } else if (row$a > 0) {
        sprintf("intercept = %.1f > 0 (sous-estime de %.1f)", row$a, row$a)
      } else {
        sprintf("intercept = %.1f < 0 (surestime de %.1f)", row$a, abs(row$a))
      }
      
      # Efficacité de la correction
      efficiency_msg <- sprintf("→ RMSD réduit de %.1f%% (%.1f → %.1f)", 
                               row$Gain_pct, row$RMSD_before, row$RMSD_after)
      
      cat(sprintf("  %s: %s; %s %s\n", 
                  row$Method, slope_msg, intercept_msg, efficiency_msg))
    }
    cat("\n")
  }
  
  # ============================================================================
  # STATISTIQUES GLOBALES
  # ============================================================================
  
  cat("=== STATISTIQUES GLOBALES ===\n\n")
  
  cat(sprintf("Réduction moyenne du RMSD: %.1f%%\n", mean(correction_factors$Gain_pct)))
  cat(sprintf("Réduction minimale: %.1f%% (%s, %s)\n", 
              min(correction_factors$Gain_pct),
              correction_factors$Method[which.min(correction_factors$Gain_pct)],
              correction_factors$Metric[which.min(correction_factors$Gain_pct)]))
  cat(sprintf("Réduction maximale: %.1f%% (%s, %s)\n\n", 
              max(correction_factors$Gain_pct),
              correction_factors$Method[which.max(correction_factors$Gain_pct)],
              correction_factors$Metric[which.max(correction_factors$Gain_pct)]))
  
  # Par métrique
  cat("Réduction moyenne par métrique:\n")
  by_metric <- correction_factors %>%
    group_by(Metric) %>%
    summarise(Mean_Gain = mean(Gain_pct),
              SD_Gain = sd(Gain_pct),
              .groups = "drop")
  for (i in 1:nrow(by_metric)) {
    cat(sprintf("  - %s: %.1f%% (±%.1f%%)\n", 
                by_metric$Metric[i], by_metric$Mean_Gain[i], by_metric$SD_Gain[i]))
  }
  cat("\n")
  
  # Par méthode
  cat("Réduction moyenne par méthode:\n")
  by_method <- correction_factors %>%
    group_by(Method) %>%
    summarise(Mean_Gain = mean(Gain_pct),
              SD_Gain = sd(Gain_pct),
              .groups = "drop")
  for (i in 1:nrow(by_method)) {
    cat(sprintf("  - %s: %.1f%% (±%.1f%%)\n", 
                by_method$Method[i], by_method$Mean_Gain[i], by_method$SD_Gain[i]))
  }
  
}

cat("\n")
cat(paste(rep("=", 70), collapse = ""), "\n")

```
